headline: Military Procurement Corruption Probe Targets RM2.4 Billion
context: MACC opened investigations into military procurement irregularities totalling RM2.4 billion across 12 defence contracts.

cards.big: MACC probes RM2.4 billion military procurement irregularities in 12 contracts
cards.sub: Investigation covers equipment purchases training and maintenance agreements

cards.big: 3 former senior defence officials arrested over RM680 million vehicle deal
cards.sub: Vehicles delivered were different specification from contracted items

cards.big: Audit found 45% price inflation across 8 purchases versus market rates
cards.sub: Intermediary companies received average 28% commission on inflated values

cards.big: MACC froze RM120 million in assets linked to procurement agents
cards.sub: Properties vehicles and accounts traced through 15 shell companies in 4 countries

cards.big: Defence corruption tests whether procurement oversight can prevent systemic waste
RM2.4 billion procurement corruption probe. Defence spending without transparent oversight invites systematic abuse.
